alerts.title: US downs Iranian drones over Strait of Hormuz amid ceasefire fragility
alerts.summary: US military shot down 2 Iranian drones above the Strait of Hormuz on 6 June, escalating tensions despite ongoing ceasefire negotiations. Multiple incidents reported near critical chokepoint per Anadolu Agensi and WQAD. Repeated drone incursions signal Iranian pressure-testing of US air defenses and threat to global shipping.

alerts.title: IAEA verification gap widens as Iran maintains 60% enrichment unmonitored
alerts.summary: UN nuclear inspectors unable to verify Iran's enriched uranium inventory since fighting escalated, per Reuters and NYT nuclear headlines. Iran continues 60% U-235 enrichment with estimated 1-2 week weapons-grade breakout window. Lack of IAEA oversight creates acute proliferation risk amid active conflict.

alerts.title: Israel and Lebanon renew ceasefire as Israeli strikes kill 12 officers in southern Lebanon
alerts.summary: Israel and Lebanon agree to ceasefire renewal amid ongoing military operations. Israeli attacks in southern Lebanon killed 12 people including high-ranking officers on 6 June per Al Jazeera. Ceasefire fragility evident with simultaneous Israeli-Lebanese strikes suggesting renewed escalation cycle.

alerts.title: Houthis intensify Red Sea shipping attacks; Iran pressure campaign confirmed
alerts.summary: Houthis conducting multiple Red Sea attacks on shipping and Israel, with confirmed reporting that Iran is pressuring group to target oil tankers. Four separate maritime incidents reported; 3 crew killed per Ahram Online, SCMP, and Courthouse News. Houthi-Iranian coordination threatens global commerce and signals escalating proxy pressure.

alerts.title: US negative sentiment reaches 54%; conflict-related keywords dominate discourse
alerts.summary: US news sentiment swung sharply negative to 54% versus 40% positive, with 'Trump,' 'war,' 'Iran,' and 'talks' topping word-frequency analysis. Shift reflects public anxiety over ongoing US-Iran hostilities and ceasefire volatility. Sentiment data from current OSINT feed shows deteriorating domestic consensus on conflict trajectory.

analysis_note: The US-Iran conflict theater remains in critical escalation phase with active kinetic operations, nuclear proliferation acceleration, and proxy coordination intensifying despite formal ceasefire talks—threat level locked at 9 (imminent)—while US arsenal depletion and regional diplomatic fragmentation compound medium-term strategic risk.
